concept: Weight clipping can limit model capacity; WGAN-GP improves stability by penalizing the critic if its gradient norm deviates from 1.

options.text: It makes the model learn complex details faster and better
It severely limits the model capacity, making it hard to learn complex images
It has no effect on the training stability of WGANs

exercises.explain: Before WGAN-GP, standard WGAN used weight clipping, which simply forced weights into a tiny box and limited how smart the model could get.
exercises.prompt: What is the main drawback of standard WGAN's weight clipping?

cards.body: Imagine putting a leash on a puppy. If you pull too hard, it can't move. In WGAN, we clipped weights to keep things stable, but it made our Critic too simple to learn details. WGAN-GP solves this!
cards.title: Unleashing the Critic
What is WGAN-GP?
cards.body: WGAN-GP (Wasserstein GAN with Gradient Penalty) replaces weight clipping. Instead of capping weights, it adds a penalty to the training loss if the Critic's gradients deviate too far from a target speed (a norm of 1).

cards.title: The Cruise Control Analogy
cards.body: Imagine driving a car down a mountain. Weight clipping is like slamming the brakes completely locked—you slide or stop. Gradient Penalty is like adaptive cruise control, keeping your speed right at the speed limit.

cards.title: Smoothing the Slopes
cards.body: If the Critic rates a fake image as 10 and a real image as 12, the slope (gradient) between them should change smoothly. If the change is too steep or too flat, the Gradient Penalty kicks in to smooth it out.

cards.title: Does Penalty Mean Slower?
cards.body: You might think penalizing gradients makes the model train slower. Actually, it allows the Critic to learn much more complex structures, leading to faster, more stable overall convergence!

cards.title: Clipping vs. Penalty

right.text: Softly penalizes bad slope speeds, keeping capacity high.

left.text: Forces all weights into a hard range, losing fine details.

cards.body: Let's compare the two ways of keeping our Critic's math in check. WGAN-GP is much gentler and smarter than Weight Clipping.

exercises.explain: WGAN-GP penalizes the Critic if the gradient norm of its outputs with respect to its inputs deviates from 1. This keeps the gradient updates smooth.

options.text: It penalizes the Critic when its gradient norm deviates from 1
It forces all weights to be exactly zero
It penalizes the Generator whenever it makes a realistic image

exercises.prompt: How does WGAN-GP maintain stable training?
WGAN-GP allows the Critic to have more capacity than standard WGAN with weight clipping.
exercises.explain: True. Gradient Penalty acts as a soft constraint on the slope of the function, leaving the weights free to adjust to represent complex features.
By avoiding weight clipping, WGAN-GP preserves model capacity so it can learn highly detailed features without collapsing.
exercises.sentence: Unlike weight clipping, WGAN-GP maintains model [blank] by penalizing gradients rather than cutting off weights.

exercises.explain: The first output shows a typical failure mode of weight clipping (blurry, repeated features), while the second showcases the sharp, stable details of WGAN-GP.
exercises.prompt: Which output showcases a GAN trained successfully with WGAN-GP over standard weight-clipped WGAN?

options.text: Option B: High-resolution faces with complex details like individual hair strands generated stably.
Option A: Low-resolution, blurry faces where all features look like a simplified average.

exercises.prompt: How does WGAN-GP avoid the capacity limits of weight clipping while keeping the training stable?
exercises.explain: In WGAN-GP, we penalize the model if the gradient norm deviates from 1, which keeps training stable while preserving capacity. Next, we will explore conditional GANs to control what the model generates.

options.text: By clipping the weights to a tiny range near zero
By penalizing the critic when its gradient norm deviates from 1
By forcing the generator to run twice as fast

title: WGAN with Gradient Penalty (WGAN-GP)
